attractions.name: Barred Owl Butcher & Table, Official Website
attractions.category: Restaurant, Bar, Butcher Shop, Barred Owl Butcher & Table, Tripadvisor
attractions.description: Barred Owl Butcher & Table is a whole-animal butcher shop and restaurant in Columbia, MO, that focuses on seasonal, locally sourced ingredients inspired by Missouri's bounty. The establishment combines a conscious effort to source responsibly with a passion for preparing delicious Midwestern-Southern American cuisine. Official Website, Show Me Missouri

attractions.region: The District (Downtown Columbia), Real Food Traveler

Extensive and innovative cocktail program with handmade bitters and mixers, Official Website
Menu influenced by a blend of Spanish, Creole, and Mediterranean flavors, COMO Magazine

attractions.name: Booches Billiard Hall
attractions.category: Bar, Restaurant, and Pool Hall Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.description: Established in 1884, Booches Billiard Hall is the oldest pool hall in Columbia and a legendary downtown staple. It is nationally renowned for its iconic, small cheeseburgers served on squares of wax paper, which have been praised by USA Today and Sports Illustrated. The establishment maintains a nostalgic, no-frills atmosphere with original pool tables and a storied history as a favorite hangout for University of Missouri students and locals alike COMO Business Times, Wikipedia.

attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Sunday: Closed Official Website, Tripadvisor
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday Official Website
attractions.admission: Free (Pool tables available for hourly rental) The Maneater
highlights: Established in 1884, making it Columbia's oldest pool hall Wikipedia
Famous for its iconic cheeseburgers served on squares of wax paper Wanderlog
Named the '#1 Greatest College Town Eat' in the nation by Sports Illustrated in 2019 Wikipedia
Inducted into the Boone County Hall of Fame in 2016 Visit Columbia MO
Features six original pool tables purchased in 1914 that are still in use COMO Business Times
Known for the iconic motto 'Closed on Sunday – See you in church!' Official Website
attractions.tips: Booches is strictly cash-only, so be sure to bring currency as credit cards are not accepted. The cheeseburgers are slider-sized, so most visitors recommend ordering at least two to three for a full meal. Expect a lively, crowded atmosphere, especially during University of Missouri game days Reddit, The Maneater.

attractions.name: Boone County Courthouse
attractions.description: The Boone County Courthouse is a historic landmark located in downtown Columbia, Missouri, serving as the seat of the 13th Judicial Circuit. As a contributing property to the Downtown Columbia Historic District, the current building is the third courthouse at this location and stands at the northern end of the Avenue of the Columns. It is known for its classic architecture and its central role in the legal and community life of Boone County (Wikipedia)).
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Saturday - Sunday: Closed (Boone County Government)
attractions.admission: Free (Boone County Government)
highlights: Seat of the 13th Judicial Circuit of Missouri (Boone County Bar Association)
Contributing property to the Downtown Columbia Historic District (Wikipedia))
Home to the historic Courthouse Plaza, a central hub for community gatherings (Boone County Government)
Features preserved columns from the previous 1847 courthouse building on the grounds (Wikipedia))
Serves as the northern terminus of the historic Avenue of the Columns (Wikipedia)
Located near the original 1821 site of Columbia's relocation (University of Missouri Library)
attractions.tips: Visitor parking is available in the parking garage across Walnut Street or at street meters along Walnut and Ash (Thomas Law Offices). The exterior Courthouse Plaza is a popular spot for local photography and community events (Boone County Government).
attractions.nearby: University of Missouri Francis Quadrangle, Stephens Lake Park, The Blue Note, MKT Nature and Fitness Trail, Ragtag Cinema (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Broadway (Boulevard)
attractions.description: Broadway is the primary thoroughfare and cultural heart of downtown Columbia, Missouri, serving as the centerpiece of the 50-square-block area known as "The District." This historic street is lined with a dense concentration of local boutiques, art galleries, and restaurants, many of which are housed in late 19th and early 20th-century buildings. As a central hub for community life, it connects the University of Missouri campus with the city’s residential core and hosts major annual events like the True/False Film Fest.
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Public Thoroughfare); Individual business hours typically range from 10:00 AM to 9:00 PM or later for nightlife.
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: The historic Tiger Hotel featuring its iconic red neon rooftop marquee COMO Magazine
Heart of 'The District,' home to over 600 local businesses and 50 blocks of shopping and dining The District
Direct connection to the University of Missouri's historic 'Avenue of the Columns' Central Bank
Home to local landmarks like the Broadway Diner and Shakespeare's Pizza Tripadvisor
Includes multiple National Register of Historic Places districts, such as the Eighth and Broadway Historic District Wikipedia
Central venue for major city festivals including the True/False Film Fest and First Fridays Jasmine Alley
attractions.tips: Park in one of the downtown garages (like the 5th and Walnut garage) where the first hour is often free; the area is best explored on foot to enjoy the historic architecture and window shopping; visit on 'First Fridays' for free gallery tours and live music Jasmine Alley.
attractions.nearby: University of Missouri Campus, Stephens Lake Park, Rock Bridge Memorial State Park, Shelter Gardens, Missouri Theatre TheTravel

attractions.name: Broadway Brewery
attractions.description: Broadway Brewery is Missouri’s premier farm-to-table brewery and restaurant located in the heart of downtown Columbia. It specializes in expertly crafted beers ranging from traditional German lagers to hazy IPAs, paired with elevated pub fare sourced from local suppliers. The establishment features both a main restaurant and a separate taproom space on 9th Street. (Official Website, VisitMO)
attractions.hours: Brewery & Restaurant: Sunday 9:30 am - 10:00 pm; Monday 4:00 pm - 12:00 am; Tuesday - Saturday 11:00 am - 12:00 am (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website)
highlights: Farm-to-table menu featuring ingredients from local Missouri farms like Patchwork Family Farms and Hemme Brothers. (Official Website)
Wide selection of house-brewed beers including year-round staples and seasonal rotations. (Official Website)
Popular Sunday brunch with a comprehensive Build-A-Bloody Bar and specialty breakfast platters. (Official Website)
Dual-location presence with the main restaurant on Broadway and the casual Taproom on 9th featuring a 16-foot garage door. (Official Website)
Regular community events including monthly jazz performances by the Columbia Jazz Orchestra and charity trivia nights. (Official Website)
Late-night menu available on Friday and Saturday nights from 10:00 p.m. to 11:30 p.m. (Official Website)
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended for the popular Sunday brunch. If you prefer a more casual bar atmosphere, visit the Taproom on 9th which offers a different draft list and vibe. (Official Website)
attractions.nearby: Peace Park, University of Missouri campus, Logboat Brewing Co., Missouri Theatre, The District (Downtown Columbia) (Reddit, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Columbia Art League
attractions.description: The Columbia Art League provides the community with a stimulating environment for experiencing the visual arts through exhibitions, education, and promotion. Established in 1959, it showcases works from local and regional artists in a gallery setting in downtown Columbia (VisitMO, Columbia Art League).
attractions.hours: Tuesday-Friday: 11:30am - 6:00pm, Saturday: 11:00am - 5:00pm, Sunday-Monday: Closed (Columbia Art League)
attractions.admission: General admission is free (Airial Travel). Special events and workshops may have associated fees (Columbia Art League).

On-site Gift Gallery selling unique handmade items (Columbia Art League)
Art education classes and mentorship programs for students and adults (Columbia Art League)

attractions.name: David R. Francis Quadrangle
attractions.description: The David R. Francis Quadrangle, colloquially known as 'The Quad,' serves as the historic and spiritual center of the University of Missouri. It is famous for its six iconic limestone columns, which are the sole remains of the university's first building, Academic Hall, after it was destroyed by fire in 1892 (Wikipedia, Visit Columbia MO).
attractions.region: Downtown Columbia / University of Missouri Campus
attractions.hours: Monday: 12:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 12:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 12:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Thursday: 12:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Friday: 12:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Saturday: 12:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Sunday: 12:00 AM - 12:00 AM (Evendo)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Columbia MO, Airial Travel)
highlights: The Columns: Six Ionic columns remaining from the original Academic Hall.
Jesse Hall: The main administration building with a towering 180-foot dome.
Thomas Jefferson's Original Tombstone: A granite obelisk gifted to Mizzou by Jefferson's heirs.
Confucius Stone Lions: Ancient sculptures from China located near the Columns.
The Residence: The oldest building on campus, serving as the home for university chancellors since 1867.
Switzler Hall: The oldest academic building on campus, featuring a bell tower.
attractions.tips: The Quad is a premier spot for photography, especially during 'golden hour' for shots of the Columns and Jesse Hall (Airial Travel). Summer evenings offer a particularly peaceful atmosphere for a stroll, and parking is conveniently available in the nearby Hitt Street or Virginia Avenue garages (Visit Columbia MO).
attractions.nearby: Downtown Columbia ('The District'), MU Student Center, Missouri School of Journalism, Peace Park (Wikipedia, Visit Columbia MO)

attractions.description: Eastside Tavern is a quaint, alternative-style bar and night club that has been a staple in Downtown Columbia since 1997. Known for its welcoming and eclectic atmosphere, it features classic horror memorabilia and "adorkable" geeky decor, attracting a diverse crowd ranging from college students to the local queer community. (Visit Columbia MO, Autostraddle)

Established in 1839 and named after former university president Elmer Ellis, it serves as a central hub for research, study, and historical preservation on campus Wikipedia, Mizzou Libraries.

attractions.name: Flat Branch Pub & Brewing
attractions.description: Established in 1994, Flat Branch Pub & Brewing is Columbia's original brewpub, housed in a historic 1927 brick warehouse that formerly served as a Hudson car dealership. It is known for its diverse menu of handmade food, award-winning craft beers, and one of the largest scotch and bourbon selections in the Midwest.

attractions.admission: Free
highlights: First brewery in Columbia since 1841, established in 1994.

For parking, the lot in front is small; use the parking garage at 6th and Cherry for a less stressful experience. Try a beer flight to sample several house brews, and don't miss the seasonal releases on the chalkboard.

attractions.name: Peace Park (McAlester Arboretum)
attractions.description: Peace Park is a four-acre greenspace and arboretum located on the northern edge of the University of Missouri campus. Originally known as McAlester Park, it was renamed in 1971 to commemorate students killed during the Kent State and Jackson State protests and remains a popular site for relaxation, community rallies, and the annual Earth Day festival. (Wikipedia), KBIA)
attractions.region: University of Missouri Campus / Downtown Columbia
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Downtown Columbia Parking)
attractions.admission: Free (Columbia Earth Day)
highlights: Stone peace symbol memorial honoring victims of the Kent State Massacre (Wikipedia))
The 'Monkey House,' a historic brick structure originally used for medical research (Wikipedia))
McAlester Arboretum, featuring over 100 trees of more than 40 different species (Wikipedia))
A scenic stone bridge across Goose Creek, central to local campus legends (COMO Magazine)
Host site for Columbia's annual Earth Day festival (COMO Magazine)
attractions.tips: The park is a favorite local spot for hammocking, slacklining, and meditation. According to campus legend, couples who walk across the stone bridge together are destined to marry. (KBIA, COMO Magazine)

attractions.name: Speaker's Circle
attractions.description: Speaker's Circle is a prominent public plaza on the University of Missouri campus designated as a "speaker's corner," where anyone is allowed to address a crowd without a permit. The site is famous for its unique acoustic properties, including a central "echo spot" that amplifies a speaker's voice to the surrounding area. It serves as a historic hub for free expression, student activism, and impromptu performances within the campus community. (University of Missouri, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: Conley Avenue & S 9th Street, Columbia, MO 65211
attractions.region: University of Missouri Campus / Downtown Columbia (Visit Columbia MO)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours daily (University of Missouri)
attractions.admission: Free (University of Missouri)
highlights: Designated as an official free speech zone and 'speaker's corner' since February 2, 1987. (University of Missouri)
Features a unique acoustic 'echo spot' in the center that amplifies the speaker's voice. (YouTube - Campus Tour)
One of only two permit-free speaking locations in the entire state of Missouri. (University of Missouri)
Central location for campus protests, religious discourse, and student demonstrations. (KBIA)
Historic site formerly known as Conley Plaza before its redesign and renaming. (University of Missouri)
Key landmark in 'The District' area, bridging the campus and downtown Columbia. (Visit Columbia MO)
attractions.tips: Stand directly in the center of the brick circle and face any direction to experience the 'echo effect' where your own voice sounds significantly louder to you. Weekdays between classes (around noon or 3 PM) are the best times to see active speakers or performances. Be prepared for a wide variety of viewpoints, as it is a completely open forum. (YouTube - Campus Tour, University of Missouri)

The park features a tram ride to the observation deck, an extensive museum, and the historic Old Courthouse. Gateway Arch Wikipedia, National Park Service

attractions.description: Ragtag Cinema is a nonprofit movie theater located in Columbia, MO, that serves as a focal point for captivating and engaging the community through immersive arts experiences. Originally founded in 1998 as an all-volunteer effort, it moved to its current home in the historic Coca-Cola bottling plant in 2008, where it shares space with Uprise Bakery and Hitt Records. Ragtag Cinema
attractions.hours: Monday - Friday: Opens at 12:00 PM; Saturday & Sunday: Opens 30 minutes before the first film. Ragtag Cinema
attractions.admission: Adult: $11.00; Member: $7.00; Matinee: $9.00; Child (12 & under): $9.00; Senior (65 & over): $9.00; Military: $9.00. Ragtag Cinema
highlights: Nonprofit independent cinema showing new, indie, international, and classic films. Ragtag Cinema
Located in the historic 1935 Coca-Cola bottling plant building known as 'Hittsville'. Ragtag Cinema
Home to the True/False Film Fest, a major documentary film festival. Ragtag Cinema
Features a 35mm change-over system for authentic film screenings. Ragtag Cinema
Fully wheelchair accessible and offers assistive technologies like CaptiView and Fidelio. Ragtag Cinema
Shares a building with Uprise Bakery and Hitt Records, offering a unique 'public house + arthouse' vibe. Ragtag Cinema
attractions.tips: Parking is free on weekends and after 6:00 PM on weekdays. Only food and drink from Uprise Bakery is allowed in the theaters. Call one hour ahead for Fidelio or CaptiView assistive technologies to arrange assistance. Ragtag Cinema, Ragtag Cinema
attractions.nearby: Uprise Bakery, Hitt Records, Sager Reeves Gallery (all within the same building). Ragtag Cinema

attractions.name: Rock Bridge Memorial State Park
attractions.description: A 2,273-acre state park known for its unique karst topography, featuring a large cave system, sinkholes, and a natural rock bridge. It offers over 15 miles of trails for hiking, bicycling, and horseback riding through scenic forests and restored grasslands.
attractions.hours: Park Grounds: Sunrise to sunset, year-round. Park Office: 9 a.m. to 3 p.m., Monday through Friday (April 15 - Oct. 31 on-season; Nov. 1 - April 14 off-season).
attractions.admission: Free (Interpretive programs and shelter reservations may have fees)
highlights: Rock Bridge: A 125-foot long natural tunnel formed by a collapsed cave roof.
Devil's Icebox: A double sinkhole with a spring and underground river, offering cool air year-round.
Connor's Cave: A 166-foot long cave that visitors can explore on their own (downstream from Devil's Icebox).
Gans Creek Wild Area: 750 acres of solitude with high bluffs and rare plants, ideal for hiking and horseback riding.
Devil's Icebox Boardwalk: A half-mile trail that passes over and under the rock bridge.
Karst Topography: Numerous sinkholes and caves illustrating Missouri\
underworld.
attractions.tips: Bring sturdy, grippy hiking shoes and water shoes if you plan to explore Connor's Cave or the rock bridge. Arrive early on weekends as parking lots at the Devil's Icebox trailhead fill quickly. Note that Devil's Icebox Cave is closed to the public to protect endangered bats, but wild cave tours may be available by registration. Bring a flashlight and helmet for cave exploration.
attractions.nearby: Three Creeks Conservation Area, Plowboy Bend Conservation Area, A. Perry Philips Park, University of Missouri (Mizzou) campus, Finger Lakes State Park, Katy Trail State Park.

attractions.name: Rose Music Hall
attractions.description: Rose Music Hall is a historic live music venue, bar, and restaurant located in downtown Columbia, MO. Originally established in 1999 as Mojos, it features both an intimate indoor stage and a large outdoor space called Rose Park, hosting a diverse array of local and national talent across various genres. (Rose Music Hall About)
attractions.region: North Village Arts District (FPC Special Events)
attractions.hours: Doors typically open 1 hour before performances; Box Office (at The Blue Note) is open Fridays 11 AM – 3 PM and on event nights starting 30 minutes prior to doors. (Rose Music Hall FAQ)
attractions.days_open: Open daily for events (check calendar) (Rose Music Hall Calendar)
attractions.admission: Varies by event (typically $10-$45; some events are Free) (TicketSmarter, Rose Music Hall Calendar)
highlights: Indoor and outdoor performance spaces (Rose Park) (Venue Maps)
Full bar and on-site food options (TicketSmarter)
Hosts the annual Summerfest and regional festivals (TicketSmarter)
Features live music, trivia, and comedy shows (Visit Columbia MO)
Available for private event rentals and weddings (FPC Special Events)
State-of-the-art sound and lighting systems (Visit Columbia MO)
attractions.tips: Arrive 30-60 minutes early for better parking; street parking is free on Sundays and after 6 PM in many downtown areas. Bring a blanket or folding chair for outdoor shows in Rose Park. (TicketSmarter, Rose Music Hall Parking, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: The Blue Note, Stephens Lake Park, and various downtown Columbia shops and galleries in the North Village Arts District (Wikipedia), Visit Columbia MO)

attractions.name: Saint Louis Zoological Park
attractions.description: The Saint Louis Zoological Park is a world-renowned destination home to over 16,000 animals representing 500 species across six distinct zones. As a leader in animal management and conservation, it offers immersive habitats and educational experiences within the heart of Forest Park (Saint Louis Zoo, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: One Government Drive, St. Louis, MO 63110-1332
attractions.hours: Spring (Feb 28 – May 21, 2026): 9 a.m. – 5 p.m. daily; Summer (May 26 – Aug 23, 2026): Sun-Thu 8 a.m. – 5 p.m., Fri-Sat 8 a.m. – 6 p.m.; Fall/Winter (Oct 8 – Dec 31, 2026): 9 a.m. – 4 p.m. daily (Saint Louis Zoo).
attractions.days_open: Open daily, including most holidays (closed Christmas Day and New Year's Day) (Saint Louis Zoo).
attractions.admission: Free general admission; paid attractions (Sea Lion Show, Zooline Railroad, etc.) range from $3.00 to $7.95 each, or can be bundled with a seasonal Adventure Pass (Saint Louis Zoo, St. Louis Magazine).
highlights: Penguin and Puffin Coast featuring both indoor and outdoor viewing areas (Wikipedia)
Sea Lion Sound with its unique walk-through underwater viewing tunnel (Saint Louis Zoo)
River's Edge, a series of naturalistic habitats for elephants, cheetahs, and hippos (Saint Louis Zoo)
The 1904 World's Fair Flight Cage, one of the world's largest free-flight aviaries (Wikipedia)
Emerson Zooline Railroad, a narrow-gauge train that circles the park (Saint Louis Zoo)
Bayer Insectarium featuring a Butterfly House and diverse invertebrate exhibits (Saint Louis Zoo)
attractions.tips: Arrive early in the morning to secure parking and see active animals; wear comfortable walking shoes; most walkways are shaded, making it manageable even on hot days; check the daily schedule for animal feeding times and sea lion performances (Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Saint Louis Art Museum, Missouri History Museum, Saint Louis Science Center, The Muny, and Turtle Playground (all within Forest Park) (Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: Shelter Gardens
attractions.description: Shelter Gardens is an award-winning five-acre botanical garden located behind the Shelter Insurance corporate headquarters in Columbia. This peaceful retreat features over 300 varieties of trees and shrubs, along with thousands of seasonal annuals and perennials. It serves as a community landmark for relaxation, education, and special events like outdoor concerts and weddings. (Shelter Insurance) (Visit Columbia MO)
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 8:00 AM - Dusk (Shelter Insurance) (Visit Columbia MO)
attractions.admission: Free (Shelter Insurance)
highlights: Replica 19th-century one-room schoolhouse (Newcomer School)
Sensory garden designed for the visually impaired
Vietnam Veterans Memorial and Inspiration Garden
Scenic waterfall, rock garden, and reflecting lily pond
Over 300 varieties of trees and shrubs and 15,000+ annuals/perennials
Free concert series held on Sunday evenings in June and July
attractions.tips: The gardens are especially vibrant during spring and summer blooms; visit on Sundays in June and July for free evening concerts. The paths are paved and wheelchair accessible, making it an easy stroll for all visitors. Note that the garden closes during inclement weather and on Christmas Day. (PlanetWare) (Shelter Insurance)
attractions.nearby: Columbia Mall, Clary-Shy Community Park (Columbia Farmers Market), Cosmo Park, Stephens Lake Park, and the University of Missouri campus (Expedia) (Lewis and Clark Trail)

attractions.name: Stephens Lake Park
attractions.description: Stephens Lake Park is the crown jewel of Columbia’s park system, featuring an 11-acre fishing and swimming lake surrounded by 116 acres of mature trees and scenic landscapes. As a Level II Accredited Arboretum, the park serves as a community hub for outdoor recreation, hosting festivals, concerts, and a variety of seasonal winter sports. (City of Columbia)
attractions.hours: Daily 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM; Fishing: 4:30 AM - 11:00 PM (City of Columbia)
attractions.admission: Free (City of Columbia)
highlights: 11-acre fishing and swimming lake with a boardwalk and beach (City of Columbia)
Level II Accredited Arboretum with diverse tree collections (City of Columbia)
Stephens Spraygrounds and multiple playgrounds (Visit CoMo)
Outdoor amphitheater for community concerts and events (City of Columbia)
Extensive trail system including a 1.7-mile perimeter trail (City of Columbia)
Winter sports center featuring a popular sledding hill (Visit CoMo)
attractions.tips: The swimming beach is unguarded and open from May 1 to September 30; users swim at their own risk (City of Columbia). Free WiFi is available near the shelters and beach area (City of Columbia). Wood is provided for the fire pit at Collins Shelter during the winter sports season (Visit CoMo).
attractions.nearby: Darwin and Axie Hindman Discovery Garden, Riechmann Indoor Pavilion, Lions-Stephens Park, University of Missouri (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: The Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art (Nelson-Atkins)
attractions.description: The Nelson-Atkins Museum of Art is a world-class cultural institution in Kansas City, Missouri, housing more than 40,000 works of art spanning 5,000 years of history. The museum is renowned for its iconic giant shuttlecock sculptures in the surrounding sculpture park and its architecturally acclaimed Bloch Building extension. It offers free general admission to its vast collections, which include significant holdings in Asian, European, and American art (Nelson-Atkins).
attractions.hours: Mon: 10am–5pm; Tue: Closed; Wed: Closed; Thu: 10am–9pm; Fri: 10am–9pm; Sat: 10am–5pm; Sun: 10am–5pm (Nelson-Atkins)
attractions.admission: General Admission: Free for all visitors; Special Exhibitions (e.g., Timeless Mucha): $13 for Public, $10 for Members; Group Rates for featured exhibitions: $6 (Nelson-Atkins, Visit KC)
highlights: Donald J. Hall Sculpture Park featuring the iconic giant shuttlecocks (Nelson-Atkins)
The Bloch Building, home to an extensive Impressionist and Post-Impressionist collection (Nelson-Atkins)
Caravaggio’s masterpiece "Saint John the Baptist in the Wilderness" (Nelson-Atkins)
Rozzelle Court Restaurant, a 15th-century-style Italian courtyard dining experience (Nelson-Atkins)
Art Course, a seasonal nine-hole mini-golf course inspired by works in the museum's collection (Nelson-Atkins)
The Tivoli Cinema, offering year-round screenings of independent and foreign films (Nelson-Atkins)
attractions.tips: Guests are encouraged to check in online or at museum kiosks to receive a required wristband. Large bags should be stored at the free coat check; backpacks must be worn on the front of the body to protect the art. Free street parking is available on Rockhill Road, while garage parking is $20 for non-members (Nelson-Atkins).
attractions.nearby: Kemper Museum of Contemporary Art, Kansas City Art Institute, Country Club Plaza (Nelson-Atkins)

attractions.name: Three Creeks Conservation Area
attractions.description: Three Creeks Conservation Area is a 1,600-acre nature preserve featuring rugged forest, scenic bluffs, intermittent streams, and geologic formations. The area is named for the three creeks that flow through it: Turkey Creek, Bass Creek, and Bonne Femme Creek. (Missouri Department of Conservation, Visit Columbia MO)
attractions.hours: 04:00 AM - 10:00 PM daily (Missouri Department of Conservation)
attractions.admission: Free (Missouri Department of Conservation)
highlights: 8-mile multi-use trail for biking, hiking, and equestrian use
3-mile interpretive hiking trail
Scenic bluffs and geologic karst formations
Hunters Cave and intermittent streams
Diverse habitats including old eastern red cedar trees and prairie communities
Historical significance with identifiable house sites from post-Civil War era
attractions.tips: Wear sturdy hiking boots as trails can be muddy and rugged. Pets must be kept on a leash at all times. Groups of more than 10 people require a special use permit for camping or organized activities. (Missouri Department of Conservation, KC Hiker)
attractions.nearby: Rock Bridge Memorial State Park, Mark Twain National Forest, Gans Creek Recreation Area, City of Ashland (KC Hiker, Missouri Division of Tourism)

attractions.name: Twin Lakes Recreation Area
attractions.description: Twin Lakes Recreation Area is a 73.3-acre community park featuring a large fishing lake, multiple off-leash dog parks, and a children's playground. It serves as a key recreational hub with access to the MKT and County House Trails. City of Columbia
attractions.address: 2500 Chapel Hill Rd, Columbia, MO 65203, United States
attractions.hours: Park: 6:00 AM – 11:00 PM; Fishing: 4:30 AM – 11:00 PM City of Columbia
attractions.days_open: Open Daily (Water play and Lakeside dog park are seasonal: April 15 - Oct 15) City of Columbia
attractions.admission: Park & Fishing: Free; Little Mates Cove (Water Park): Closed until renovations are completed (Historically paid admission). City of Columbia FAQ, Visit Columbia MO
highlights: 18.8-acre fishing lake stocked with largemouth bass, bluegill, and channel catfish
Three distinct off-leash dog areas: Lakeside, Paws Pond (small dogs), and Off-Season area
Largest reservable outdoor picnic shelter in the city park system
Direct trail connections to the scenic MKT Trail and County House Trail
Accessible ADA-compliant fishing dock and playground area
Non-motorized boating access (carry-in only) on the main lake
attractions.tips: Dog owners should use the Off-Season Dog Park from December through February to prevent pets from falling through lake ice. The Lakeside and Paws Pond areas are closed during winter months to protect the turf. City of Columbia
attractions.nearby: Shelter Gardens, Rock Bridge Memorial State Park, Stephens Lake Park, Mizzou Arena, Faurot Field, Columbia Mall. Tripadvisor
